Han Dang (Yigong)
韓當 (義公)

Han Dang, styledYigong, was fromLingzhi[令支],Liaoxi[遼西].A
He was good at shooting while riding a horse, and he had physicalstrength.B
Ling [令] has the fanqie of Lang [郎] and Ding [定]; Zhi [支] has the fanqie of Ju [巨] and Er [兒].
TheBook of Wustates: Through bitter labor,[Han] Dangrendered merits, but because he was a military servant, it was given to the heroic and mighty, and he thus was not given peerage or title. Only during the end of the time of[Sun] Jianwas he madeMajor of a Separate Unit.
He was favored bySun Jian, and he followed in conquests and military campaigns. He found himself in perilous situations several times, charging into the enemies and capturing prisoners. He was madeMajor of a Separate Unit. WhenSun Ceferried across to the east, he followed in the suppression of three commanderies.1He was promoted toColonel who Ascends First, given two thousand troops, and five hundred cavalry. He followed in the conquest ofLiu Xun, the defeat ofHuang Zu, and returned to suppressPoyang[鄱陽], becomingChiefofLe’an[樂安長]. The Shanyue submitted out of awe. Later, he, asHousehold General, resisted and defeatedExcellency Caoalong withZhou Yuand some others. He also tookNan commandery[南郡] withLü Meng. He was promoted toYongningand becameGrand AdministratorofYongchang[永昌太守]. At the battle ofYidu[宜都之役], he,Lu Xun, andZhu Ran, among others, all attacked theShuarmy at Zhouxiang [涿鄉] and crushed them. He was promoted toGeneral of Might and Fiercenessand enfeoffed asMarquis of a Capital District.•••
The “three commanderies” presumably refer toWu commandery,Danyang commandery, andKuaiji commandery.↩
WhenCao ZhenattackedNan commandery[南郡],[Han] Dangprotected the south-east. When he was commanding outside, he sternly ordered his generals and soldiers to be of the selfsame mind and to defend firmly. He also respected and looked up to his commanders and superiors, obeying laws and commands.[Sun] Quansaw this as good. In the second year of theera [223], he was enfeoffed asMarquisofShicheng[石城侯], promoted toGeneral who Illuminates Strength, andGrand AdministratorofGuanjun[冠軍太守].2He later also was given the title ofCommander. He led ten thousand Death Darer and Trouble Reliever troops, suppressing the rebels ofDanyang[丹楊] and defeatingthem.•••
Guanjunwas a county, not a commandery. The title ofGrand Administratorwas supposed to govern over commanderies. Because of that, this may have been a honorary title forHan Dang, especially asWudid not controlNanyang commandery, whereGuanjun countywas in.↩
It happened that he died of illness. His son,[Han] Zong, succeeded the marquisate and took over the troops. That year [226],[Sun] Quanwent in an expedition againstShiyang. Because[Han] Zongwas mourning, he sent him to guardWuchang[武昌]. However,[Han] Zongwas promiscuous and violated the law. Even though[Sun] Quandid not make any inquiry because of his father,[Han] Zongfelt afraid.CHe thus loaded the remains of his father and led his mother, family members, and subordinates, all of whom numbered in the thousands, to flee toWei.Weiappointed him as General and enfeoffed him asMarquisofGuangyang[廣陽侯]. He often raided the borders and killed the people there; thus,[Sun] Quanoften clenched his teeth. In the battle ofDongxing[東興之役],[Han] Zongwas the vanguard, but the army was defeated and he died.Zhuge Kebeheaded him and delivered his head to be shown at the temple of[Sun] Quan.3•••
Sun Quanwas dead by the timeHan Zongwas killed in battle.↩
TheBook of Wustates:[Han] Zongwanted to rebel, but feared that his attendants would not join in, so he urged them into plundering and raiding, then he showed that he intended to absolve them. Thus they followed that example continuously, being a major menace wherever they went. Later, he lied that he received an edict, which stated that his subordinates, being bandits, should be interrogated and reproached. He said, “The officers, officials, and their inferiors would all be arrested and punished.” He also said, “I fear that I will be punished.” His attendants thus said, “Then we must flee!” Thus, they plotted a strategy together, that he would bury his father as a way to call all of his relatives and aunts. He then had them all married to his officers and officials. He had all of his favored maids and concubines gifted to those he was intimate and close with. He killed oxen, drank wine, and smeared blood on their faces, swearing an oath of alliance with them.

Han Dang (Yigong)
韓當 (義公)

Han Dang, styled Yigong, was from Lingzhi in Liaoxi. Adept at archery and riding, and possessing great strength in his arms, he was favoured by Sun Jian. Following Sun Jian he fought near and far, encountering great danger not only once, battling deep in enemy territory and capturing captives; and thus he was made Major with a Separate CommandA. When Sun Ce sailed east, Han Dang followed him to fight for the three commanderies [that were to be the basis of Wu], and was promoted to Colonel, and given two thousand soldiers and fifty horses. He then followed him to wage war against Liu Xun, and then to defeat Huang Zu. Upon his return he fought for Poyang. After that he was made to Chief of Le’an prefecture, all the Shanyue people succumbed to him. Later on, as a General of the Inner Guard, and along with Zhou Yu and others, Han Dang defended against Cao Cao’s invasion and defeated him. Then he attacked and and took over Nanjun along with Lü Meng, and was promoted to Lieutenant-General and Grand Administrator or Yongchang. During the battle of Yidu, Han Dang, Lu Xun, Zhu Ran amongst others attacked the Shu army at Zhuoxiang and won a great victory over them. And so he was given the title of General of Majesty and Courage, and was made a Marquis of a Chief Commune. When Cao Zhen attacked Nanjun, Han Dang was protecting the southeast.
InHistory of Wu: Han Dang was diligent and won many merits, but as he gave all his captives to other generals, his rank was not increased.1He was only made Major with a Separate Command at the end of Jian’stime.•••
Since martial achievements were partially measured according to the number of enemy soldiers captured, Han Dang allowed his cohorts to advance faster than him by giving his captives to them.↩
Whenever Han Dang was out leading troops, he inspired all his captains and soldiers to be of one heart and to put up a stout defense. Furthermore, he showed respect and obedience to his superiors and followed the rules and orders to the word, and so Sun Quan thought well of him. In the second year of Huangwu (AD 223), Han Dang was made Marquis of Shicheng, and promoted to the rank of General Who Shines in his Firmness, with the additional assignment of Grand Administrator of Guanjun. Later on the title of Chief Controller was also added to him. Once he led ten thousand dare-to-dies and relieve troops to fight the bandits at Danyang, and achieved victory. Soon after that, he died of illness, and his son Zong inherited the marquisate and military command.
In that year, Sun Quan went to wage war at Shiyang, and as Han Zong was in mourning,2he let him stay behind to guard Wuchang. However, Han Zong lived in debauchery and great depravity. Although Sun Quan, for the sake of Han Dang, did not demand an account of Han Zong’s actions, Zong was fearful insideB; and so, while still in his mourning period, he fled to Wei, taking several thousands of people from his mother’s family and his retainers with him. Wei made him a general and Marquis of Guangyang. Many times he assaulted the boarder [of Wu], killing the common folk there, causing Sun Quan to loathe him. During the battle of Dongxing, Zong was the vanguard, but his troops were vanquished and he died in the battle. Zhuge Ke cut off his head and returned to sacrifice it at Sun Quan’stemple.•••
Traditional Confucian code requires the observation of a 3-year mourning period for the death of a father, during which one stays in guard of the grave.↩
InHistory of Wu: Han Zong wanted to rebel against Wu, but was afraid that his subordinates wouldn’t follow him. And so he pardoned them even when they ridiculed the imperial messengers and robbed and plundered. However, this became a trend and was threatening the discipline of his troops. Later, it was rumoured that he was being summoned to Sun Quan, and that he was going to be reprimanded for his subordinates’ thieving. So he said, “Those below the rank of general should be controlled.” He also feared that the crimes would implicate him, and so those around him said to him, “The only thing to do now is to leave.” They then plotted together, and using the pretense of burying his father, Han Zong had all his female relatives come, and married them off to his subordinate generals. The maids and concubines that he liked he also gave to those who were close to him. Together with his officers, they killed the sacrificial ox, and swore an oath by drinking the wine and smearing their lips with the blood of the ox.
